rejected_candidates.title: GatewaySend destination `onCall` ignores `transferFrom` result and continues as if funds were received
rejected_candidates.reason: Duplicate of existing F-005, which already covers untrusted payload accounting plus ignored ERC20 transferFrom result in `GatewaySend.onCall`.

rejected_candidates.title: Unbounded receiver bytes are persisted in refund records, enabling revert-callback gas exhaustion
rejected_candidates.reason: Not reportable as stated: upstream gateway enforces message/revert size caps, and no concrete protocol-wide gas-exhaustion path was substantiated.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewayTransferNative exposes an ETH-sentinel path in `withdrawToNativeChain` that reverts at fee transfer
rejected_candidates.reason: Subset/variant of existing F-004 (underfunded sentinel path draining reserves); separate liveness framing is not distinct root cause.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing deadline validation in GatewaySend swap execution
rejected_candidates.reason: `_doMixSwap` forwards deadline to DODO router, which is expected to enforce it; no independent deadline bypass was demonstrated in this code.

rejected_candidates.title: No minimum output amount enforcement on source-chain swaps
rejected_candidates.reason: `minReturnAmount` is part of user-supplied swap params and enforced by the downstream swap; absence of extra wrapper checks is not a standalone vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Out-of-bounds calldata read in decodePackedMessage
rejected_candidates.reason: Malformed length fields cause a revert on that call path rather than exploitable memory corruption or cross-user fund impact.

rejected_candidates.title: Predictable externalId enables front-running on source chains
rejected_candidates.reason: `externalId` is an identifier/event key, not an authorization primitive; predictability does not create direct theft or protocol manipulation by itself.

rejected_candidates.title: Gas stipend limitation in refund ETH transfers
rejected_candidates.reason: Factually incorrect: `TransferHelper.safeTransferETH` uses `.call` without a 2300-gas stipend limit.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing access control on GatewayTransferNative.withdraw function
rejected_candidates.reason: Already captured by existing F-008, where exploitability depends on residual gateway allowances and contract balances.

rejected_candidates.title: Platform fee can be set to zero enabling free withdrawals
rejected_candidates.reason: Governance/owner-configurable economic parameter, not a security flaw in permissioning or accounting.

rejected_candidates.title: Incorrect amountIn calculation in swap finalization
rejected_candidates.reason: The cited check is conservative and may only over-revert in edge cases; no realistic theft, insolvency, or permissionless DoS exploit was shown.
